sanskrit:
कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम्।
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम्॥
transliteration:
kṛṣi-go-rakṣya-vāṇijyaṃ vaiśya-karma svabhāva-jam
paricaryātmakaṃ karma śūdrasyāpi svabhāva-jam
literal_meaning: Ploughing, cow-protection, trade—these are the duties born of the nature of a vaiśya; service-oriented work is the duty born of the nature of a śūdra.
contextual_meaning: Krishna normalizes diverse life-roles by mapping them to innate temperaments, reducing shame about status and inviting self-acceptance.
psychological_state: Arjuna feels permission to stop comparing himself with others and to honor his own kṣatriya nature while respecting every temperament.
trigger_scenario: A junior employee scrolling LinkedIn feels 'less-than' beside entrepreneurs, then remembers that steady service is also sacred.
anchor_text: Honor your nature; every role serves the whole.
